---
title: "Bulk Data Download Script (download_all_data.py)"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

`inst/python/download_all_data.py` is a standalone Python script that bulk-downloads
all available election data from every supported source and saves the results as CSV
files under `data/`.  It is the fastest way to populate a local copy of the full
dataset without writing any R or Python code yourself.

All commands below assume you are in `inst/python/` with the virtual environment
activated:

```bash
cd inst/python
source ~/.virtualenvs/downballotR/bin/activate
```

---

## Quick Reference

| Goal | Command |
|---|---|
| Download everything | `python download_all_data.py` |
| One section only | `python download_all_data.py --section <name>` |
| One state (ElectionStats) | `python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state new_york` |
| Preview without downloading | `python download_all_data.py --dry-run` |
| Georgia — all years, state + county | `python download_all_data.py --section georgia` |
| Georgia — 2024 only, state totals only | `python download_all_data.py --section georgia --ga-year-from 2024 --ga-year-to 2024 --ga-level state` |
| Georgia — with vote-method breakdown | `python download_all_data.py --section georgia --vote-methods` |
| Connecticut — all years, state + town | `python download_all_data.py --section connecticut` |
| Connecticut — single year | `python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-year-from 2024 --ct-year-to 2024` |
| Connecticut — statewide totals only | `python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-level state` |
| Indiana — all years | `python download_all_data.py --section indiana` |
| Louisiana — all years | `python download_all_data.py --section louisiana` |

---

## Command-line Options

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--section` | `all` | Which section to run. See [Sections](#sections) below. |
| `--state` | all states | Restrict `election_stats` to one state (e.g. `new_york`). |
| `--workers N` | `1` | Number of parallel download threads. See [Parallelism](#parallelism). |
| `--output-dir PATH` | `<repo>/data/` | Root folder for output CSV files. |
| `--dry-run` | off | Print tasks without downloading anything. |

### Georgia-specific options

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--ga-year-from YEAR` | `2012` | First year to download for Georgia. |
| `--ga-year-to YEAR` | current year | Last year to download for Georgia. |
| `--ga-level` | `all` | `all` — state + county; `state` — statewide totals only (much faster); `county` — county-level only. |
| `--vote-methods` | off | Also capture per-contest vote-method breakdowns (Advanced Voting / Election Day / Absentee by Mail / Provisional). Adds extra Playwright clicks per page; significantly slower. |
| `--county-workers N` | `4` | Parallel Chromium browsers for county scraping. Each is a separate process — keep ≤ 6 to avoid exhausting memory. |

### Connecticut-specific options

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--ct-year-from YEAR` | `2016` | First year to download for Connecticut. |
| `--ct-year-to YEAR` | current year | Last year to download for Connecticut. |
| `--ct-level` | `all` | `all` — statewide + town; `state` — statewide totals only (much faster, no town scraping); `town` — town-level only. |
| `--ct-town-workers N` | `2` | Parallel Chromium browsers for town scraping (one per county). Keep ≤ 4 to avoid memory exhaustion. |

### Indiana-specific options

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--in-year-from YEAR` | `2019` | First year to download for Indiana. |
| `--in-year-to YEAR` | current year | Last year to download for Indiana. |
| `--in-level` | `all` | `all` — state + county; `state` or `county` only. |

### Louisiana-specific options

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--la-year-from YEAR` | `1982` | First year to download for Louisiana. |
| `--la-year-to YEAR` | `2024` | Last year to download for Louisiana. |
| `--la-level` | `all` | `all` — state + parish; `state` or `parish` only. |
| `--la-parish-workers N` | `2` | Parallel Chromium browsers for parish scraping. |

---

## Sections {#sections}

Pass one of the following names to `--section`, or omit to run all.

### `election_stats`

Scrapes ElectionStats / Civera sites for seven states.

```bash
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats
```

| State | Years | Granularity |
|---|---|---|
| Virginia | 1789–2025 | State, County |
| Massachusetts | 1970–2026 | State, County |
| Colorado | 1902–2024 | State, County |
| New Hampshire | 1970–2024 | State, County |
| South Carolina | 2008–2025 | State, County |
| New Mexico | 2000–2024 | State, County |
| New York | 1994–2024 | State, County |

**Output:**
```
data/election_stats/{state}/{state}_{year_from}_{year_to}_state.csv
data/election_stats/{state}/{state}_{year_from}_{year_to}_county.csv
```

**Single state:**
```bash
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state colorado
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state new_york
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state virginia
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state massachusetts
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state new_hampshire
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state south_carolina
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state new_mexico
```

> **Note:** South Carolina, New Mexico, and New York use a Playwright headless
> browser backend. Keep `--workers 1` when scraping those states.

---

### `nc`

Scrapes the NC State Board of Elections ZIP pipeline for precinct-level results.

```bash
python download_all_data.py --section nc
```

| Years | Granularity |
|---|---|
| 2000–2025 | Precinct, County, State |

**Output:**
```
data/northcarolina/nc_2000_2025_precinct.csv
data/northcarolina/nc_2000_2025_county.csv
data/northcarolina/nc_2000_2025_state.csv
```

---

### `georgia`

Scrapes the Georgia Secretary of State election results site for all elections
from 2012 to the present.  The site is a JavaScript-rendered Angular SPA
(results.sos.ga.gov); all scraping uses a headless Chromium browser via Playwright.

Each year's download collects every election held in that year (primaries, runoffs,
generals) and optionally scrapes all 159 counties in parallel.

```bash
# All years (2012–present), state + county totals
python download_all_data.py --section georgia

# Recent years only
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --ga-year-from 2020

# Single year
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --ga-year-from 2024 --ga-year-to 2024

# Statewide totals only — skips all 159-county scrape (much faster)
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --ga-level state

# Include vote-method breakdown (Advanced Voting / Election Day / Absentee / Provisional)
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --vote-methods

# All options combined
python download_all_data.py --section georgia \
    --ga-year-from 2020 --ga-year-to 2024 \
    --ga-level all \
    --vote-methods \
    --county-workers 4
```

| Years | Granularity | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| 2012–present | State, County | Candidate results, party, incumbency, vote totals, pct, election date, result status, vote-for-N, localities reporting |

**Output:**
```
data/georgia/ga_{year}_state.csv          # statewide candidate totals
data/georgia/ga_{year}_county.csv         # per-county candidate totals
```

With `--vote-methods`, additionally:
```
data/georgia/ga_{year}_vote_method_state.csv   # per-method statewide
data/georgia/ga_{year}_vote_method_county.csv  # per-method by county
```

> **Performance notes:**
>
> - County scraping spawns up to `--county-workers` (default 4) Chromium processes
>   in parallel per election.  A single year with several elections can take 20–60
>   minutes depending on network speed.  Use `--ga-level state` to skip county scraping
>   when only statewide totals are needed.
>
> - `--vote-methods` adds a Playwright click-and-re-render step for every contest
>   panel (50–125+ per page).  Budget an extra 5–15 minutes per election page.

---

### `connecticut`

Scrapes the Connecticut Election Management System (CTEMS) at
`ctemspublic.tgstg.net` for all elections from 2016 to the present.  CTEMS is
a JavaScript-rendered AngularJS SPA; all scraping uses a headless Chromium
browser via Playwright.

```bash
# All years (2016–present), state + town totals
python download_all_data.py --section connecticut

# Recent years only
python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-year-from 2020

# Single year
python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-year-from 2024 --ct-year-to 2024

# Statewide totals only — skips all-town scrape (much faster)
python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-level state
```

| Years | Granularity | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| 2016–present | State, Town | Candidate results, party, office, election level (Federal/State/Local), vote totals, vote pct, contest outcome |

**Output:**
```
data/connecticut/ct_{year}_state.csv   # statewide totals
data/connecticut/ct_{year}_town.csv    # per-town results with election_level
```

---

### `indiana`

Scrapes the Indiana voters portal for General Election results.

```bash
python download_all_data.py --section indiana
python download_all_data.py --section indiana --in-year-from 2024 --in-year-to 2024
```

| Years | Granularity |
|---|---|
| 2019–present | State, County |

**Output:**
```
data/indiana/in_{year}_state.csv
data/indiana/in_{year}_county.csv
```

---

### `louisiana`

Scrapes the Louisiana Secretary of State Graphical election results site.

```bash
python download_all_data.py --section louisiana
python download_all_data.py --section louisiana --la-year-from 2024 --la-year-to 2024
```

| Years | Granularity |
|---|---|
| 1982–present | State, Parish |

**Output:**
```
data/louisiana/la_{year}_state.csv
data/louisiana/la_{year}_parish.csv
```

---

## Parallelism {#parallelism}

`--workers N` runs up to N tasks simultaneously using Python threads.

```bash
# ElectionStats classic states only — safe to parallelise
python download_all_data.py --section election_stats --state virginia --workers 1

# Georgia — county-level parallelism is controlled per-election by --county-workers
# The year loop itself is always sequential (--workers has no effect on georgia)
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --county-workers 4
```

**Recommended worker counts:**

| Section | Recommended `--workers` | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `election_stats` (VA / MA / CO / NH) | 4 | HTTP only — safe |
| `election_stats` (includes SC / NM / NY) | 1 | Playwright — not thread-safe |
| `nc` | 1 | Single large ZIP pipeline |
| `georgia` | 1 (use `--county-workers` instead) | County parallelism is internal |
| `connecticut` | 1 (use `--ct-town-workers` instead) | Town parallelism is internal |
| `indiana` | 1 | Sequential year loop |
| `louisiana` | 1 (use `--la-parish-workers` instead) | Parish parallelism is internal |

> **Why the limit?** South Carolina, New Mexico, and New York (ElectionStats) all use a
> Playwright headless Chromium browser. Playwright's synchronous API is not thread-safe,
> so running multiple Playwright tasks in parallel can cause crashes.

---

## Output Directory Layout

```
data/
  election_stats/
    virginia/
      virginia_1789_2025_state.csv
      virginia_1789_2025_county.csv
    massachusetts/
      ...
    colorado/  new_hampshire/  south_carolina/  new_mexico/  new_york/

  northcarolina/
    nc_2000_2025_precinct.csv
    nc_2000_2025_county.csv
    nc_2000_2025_state.csv

  georgia/
    ga_2012_state.csv
    ga_2012_county.csv
    ...
    ga_2024_state.csv
    ga_2024_county.csv
    ga_2024_vote_method_state.csv    # only with --vote-methods
    ga_2024_vote_method_county.csv   # only with --vote-methods

  connecticut/
    ct_2016_state.csv
    ct_2016_town.csv
    ...
    ct_2024_state.csv
    ct_2024_town.csv

  indiana/
    in_2019_state.csv
    in_2019_county.csv
    ...

  louisiana/
    la_1982_state.csv
    la_1982_parish.csv
    ...
```

---

## Re-running After Failures

The script **skips files that already exist**.  If a run is interrupted or a
year fails, simply re-run the same command — completed years will be skipped
and only missing files will be downloaded.

```bash
# Resume a partial Georgia download
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --ga-year-from 2012

# Resume a partial Connecticut download
python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-year-from 2016
```

To force a re-download, delete the relevant CSV file first.

---

## Dry Run

Preview all tasks without downloading anything:

```bash
python download_all_data.py --dry-run
python download_all_data.py --section georgia --ga-year-from 2022 --vote-methods --dry-run
python download_all_data.py --section connecticut --ct-year-from 2020 --ct-level state --dry-run
```

---

## Data Coverage Summary

| Source | States/Scope | Years | Granularity | Contents |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ElectionStats | Virginia | 1789–2025 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| ElectionStats | Massachusetts | 1970–2026 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| ElectionStats | Colorado | 1902–2024 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| ElectionStats | New Hampshire | 1970–2024 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| ElectionStats | South Carolina | 2008–2025 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| ElectionStats | New Mexico | 2000–2024 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| ElectionStats | New York | 1994–2024 | State, County | Candidate results + county vote counts |
| NC State Board | North Carolina | 2000–2025 | Precinct, County, State | Raw precinct results aggregated up |
| GA SOS | Georgia | 2012–present | State, County | Candidate results, incumbency, vote totals, pct, election metadata |
| GA SOS (vote methods) | Georgia | 2012–present | State, County | Per-method vote counts (Advanced / Election Day / Absentee / Provisional) |
| CT CTEMS | Connecticut | 2016–present | State, Town | Candidate results, party, election level (Federal/State/Local), vote totals, pct, contest outcome |
| Indiana voters portal | Indiana | 2019–present | State, County | General Election candidate results |
| Louisiana SOS | Louisiana | 1982–present | State, Parish | Statewide + parish candidate results |
